children.name: Caprimulgus
children.description: Superbly cryptic nocturnal birds that roost motionless on forest floors by day, invisible among dead leaves and pine needles. At dusk they produce a loud mechanical churring that can be heard for kilometres over Swedish heathland. They catch flying moths and beetles in a wide gaping mouth fringed with rictal bristles.

children.name: Caprimulgus europaeus
children.description: A masterfully camouflaged nocturnal insectivore that lies motionless along a branch by day, its bark-like plumage rendering it almost invisible. At dusk males deliver a sustained mechanical churring over Swedish heaths and clearings.
